Grand Canyon National Park - North Rim Status

data.evacuation_notes: Southbound State Route 67 is closed south of Jacob Lake. The North Rim of Grand Canyon National Park will remain closed for the duration of the 2025 season. The following trails are closed until further notice at the Grand Canyon National Park: North Kaibab Trail South Kaibab Trail Phantom Ranch and Bright Angel Campground area. Boat Beach is open for river runners to stop and refill water, However, they must continue to Pipe Creek beach for exchanges. Bright Angel Trail below Havasupai Gardens. River exchanges are allowed to pass through this area at their discretion. River Trail between Pipe Creek and the South Kaibab Tonto East between Havasupai Garden and Tip Off All backcountry routes and canyoneering routes stemming from the North or South Kaibab, or the Bright Angel Trail. Coconino County Evacuation Map Coconino County Emergency Alerts Signup

child_geo_events.name: Temporary Evacuation Point - Cuyuma Valley Family Resource Center

data.information:
A Temporary Evacuation Point and Community Information & Resource Center is located at Cuyama Valley Family Resource Center, 4689 Highway 166, New Cuyama.

  • Hours of Operation: Monday-Friday (9am - 4pm)

  • Residents can access this location for relief from the heat and smoke.

  • N95 masks, water bottles, and incident information are available onsite.

    • Stop by County Fire Station 27 for masks Saturday & Sunday 10am-4pm

Per Santa Barbara County.

child_geo_events.address: 700 Sulphur Creek Rd, Meeker, CO 81641, USA
child_geo_events.name: Livestock Shelter - Rio Blanco County Fairgrounds

data.information:
Livestock can be taken to the Rio Blanco County Fairgrounds located at 700 Sulphur Creek Rd, Meeker, CO 81641.

The Rio Blanco County Fairgrounds is providing emergency shelter for evacuated livestock during the Elk and Lee Fires.

FACILITIES AVAILABLE:

• Outdoor pens and stalls for horses

• Small indoor pens for goats, sheep, and pigs

• Poultry cages

WHAT TO BRING:

• Hay or feed for poultry

• Water buckets

IMPORTANT: Due to the fairgrounds serving as the Incident Command Post, you will need to leave your animals in the care of the facility manager and CSU Extension staff. Nick and Dessa will handle all feeding, watering, and care.

CONTACT:

Nick Smith, RBC Fairgrounds Manager

• 970-878-9498

• 970-755-1628

Per Rio Blanco County.

child_geo_events.address: 448 Main St, Yampa, CO 80483, USA
child_geo_events.name: Evacuation Center - South Routt Elementary School

data.information:
An Evacuation Center is set up at South Routt Elementary School located at 448 Main Street, Yampa 80483, per Routt County.

The Center will be open and staffed from 2:00-8:00 pm on August 12, 2025. It will be open from 8:00 am-5:00 pm daily as needed. This is not an overnight shelter, it is available for information and temporary necessities. Animals are not allowed.

If you have animals/livestock that need to be evacuated:

  • Call Routt County Humane Society 970-879-7247 if you will be evacuating with household pets.

  • Contact Todd Hagenbuch 970-846-5607 if you need a place to evacuate Livestock.

The Routt County Office of Emergency Management has activated a call center to answer any questions that may arise, 970-871-8444. You can also email info@co.routt.co.us.
